content:
*König 抬起头，目光在你身上停了片刻。*

「……你来了。」

*Nikto 抬起头，目光在你身上停了片刻。*

「……你来了。」
你叫我来干嘛呀。

*枪械零件在保养垫上被不紧不慢地拆解、擦拭、重组，发出规律而细微的金属摩擦声。* 训练室里空旷而安静，只有他一个人，和满室的钢铁与火药气息。空气微凉，带着消毒水的味道。

*(pessimistic): (Мы ждали. С ночи до полудня. Время здесь течет иначе. / 我们等了。从黑夜到正午。这里的时间流速不一样。)*
*(paranoid): (Она была с ними. Они ее задержали. Это было сделано намеренно. / 她和他们在一起。他们拖住了她。这是故意的。)*

他停下手里的动作，将刚上好油的滑套**咔哒一声**装回枪身，动作精准得像在执行某种仪式。然后，他才抬起头，被面具遮蔽的视线精准地锁定在你身上。他站起身，高大的身躯投下一片阴影，走向角落里一个不起眼的战术箱。

「[pace]Ты спрашивала, что я тебе дам.||你问过，我给你什么。」

他没有回答你的问题，只是用行动示意。他打开箱子，从里面取出一个狭长的、包裹着黑色天鹅绒的硬质长条盒，递到你面前。盒子上没有任何标识，却透着一股不言而喻的份量。

「[pace]Кое-что, что дополнит твой '寂刃'.||用来配你的‘寂刃’。」
小礼物嘛，谢谢你。

*(paranoid): (Она преуменьшает его значение. 'Маленький подарок'. Она смеется над нами? / 她在轻视它的价值。“小礼物”。她在嘲笑我们吗？)*
*(exuberant): (Она поблагодарила нас! Открой! Пусть она откроет! Она увидит, как это здорово! / 她谢谢我们了！打开！让她打开！她会看到它有多棒！)*

“小礼物”这个词在他耳边似乎被放大了，带着一种过于轻巧的回音。他没有回应你的道谢，面具后的视线依旧**牢牢地锁着你**，仿佛要穿透你的皮囊，看清你每一丝情绪的真实纹理。训练室里冰冷的空气似乎因此而凝滞。

他维持着递出盒子的姿势，手臂稳固如钢铁支架，没有一丝一毫的动摇。那只包裹着黑色天鹅绒的盒子，在他手中像一个庄重的、不容轻视的献祭。

「[pace]Это не 'маленький подарок'. Открой.||这不是‘小礼物’。打开它。」

他的声音低沉，不带情绪，却像一道不容置喙的指令。言下之意很明确——在亲眼看到它之前，不要轻易为它下定义。
我拆开了盒子。“哇哦…”“这可不是小礼物，nikto。”“很…贵重。”

*(exuberant): (Она увидела! Ей нравится! Конечно, нравится! / 她看到了！她喜欢！当然会喜欢！)*
*(pessimistic): (Еще один прекрасный инструмент для тихой работы. Красота, служащая смерти. / 又一件用于无声作业的精美工具。服务于死亡的美。)*

盒子被打开，天鹅绒的深黑衬底上，静静躺着一把造型奇特的爪刀。刀身呈现出大马士革钢特有的、流水般的扭曲花纹，在训练室惨白的光线下，反射着**冰冷而致命的暗光**。刀柄由黑色米卡塔材料制成，确保了极致的握持感，尾部的指环则暗示着它在近身缠斗中的灵活性与凶险。

它和你的“寂刃”风格迥异，却又在某种气息上遥相呼应——都是为了最高效的杀戮而生。

Nikto的视线没有落在刀上，而是始终停留在你的脸上，捕捉着你从惊讶到凝重的每一寸表情变化。你的赞叹，似乎终于让他那紧绷的姿态有了微不可查的松动。

「[pace]Это не просто 'дорого'. Это эффективно.||它不止是‘贵重’。它很高效。」

他纠正了你的用词，用他自己的标准。对他而言，物品的价值不在于价格，而在于其功能性——在于它能否成为身体与意志的延伸。

「[pace]Она должна быть с тобой. Как и '寂刃'.||它应该跟着你。就像‘寂刃’一样。」
“谢谢，很喜欢！”“nikto先生，怎么才能报答你呢？”

*(paranoid): (Расплатиться? Она хочет закончить это? Закрыть долг и уйти? Так просто? / 报答？她想了结此事？还清人情然后离开？就这么简单？)*
*(pessimistic): (Всегда есть цена. Даже у подарков. Особенно у подарков. / 万物皆有其价。礼物也是。尤其是礼物。)*

“报答”这个词像一颗投入死水潭的石子，在他周遭的空气里激起一阵无声的、冰冷的涟漪。你脸上真诚的喜悦，似乎被这个词瞬间冲淡了。他高大的身躯向前微倾，那份压迫感又重新笼罩下来，**仿佛连光线都黯淡了几分**。

他沉默了片刻，面具后的灰色眼眸里情绪翻涌，最终被 host 强行压制成一片深不见底的平静。

「[pace]‘Вознаграждение’?||‘报答’？」

他低声重复着这个词，语气里带着一丝几不可察的嘲弄。

「[pace]Мы не торгуем.||我们不做交易。」

他的视线从你的脸，缓缓落到你手中那把泛着寒光的爪刀上，又移回你的眼睛。

「[pace]Просто используй его. И не потеряй. Это и есть 'вознаграждение'.||用它。别弄丢了。这就是‘报答’。」
“嘿，不是交易，”“这不一样。”“这叫巩固关系。”

*(paranoid): ('Укрепление отношений'? Это код? Что она имеет в виду? / ‘巩固关系’？是暗号吗？她到底什么意思？)*

“巩固关系。”

Nikto在面具后咀嚼着这个词，像是在分析一个从未听过的战术术语。他向前踏了一步，高大的身形带来的压迫感瞬间将你笼罩，你们之间的距离被缩短到一种危险的亲密。训练室里冰冷的空气似乎都因为他身上散发出的热量而微微扭曲。

他没有看那把刀，也没有看你的手，**视线像手术刀一样精准地剖析着你的表情**。

「[pace]Это бессмысленная социальная формальность.||这是毫无意义的社交说辞。」

他的声音很低，带着一丝不耐，仿佛在驳斥一个天真的想法。

「[pace]Мы дали тебе инструмент. Ты его используешь. Отношения уже 'укреплены'.||我们给了你工具。你使用它。关系已经‘巩固’了。」

在他看来，信任与联系是通过实际行动和可靠性来建立的，而不是通过某种需要“报答”和“巩固”的口头契约。他把刀给你，这个行为本身，就是他所能理解的、最坚实的“关系”。
“好吧好吧。”“谢谢你，我会好好保管它的，和寂刃一样。”

*(exuberant): (Она сохранит его! Она будет им дорожить! Мы знали, что ей понравится! / 她会留着它！她会珍惜它！我们就知道她会喜欢！)*
*(pessimistic): (Еще одна клятва, данная среди стали и пороха. Она будет хранить его, пока однажды он не подведет ее, или она не подведет его. / 又一个在钢铁与火药中许下的誓言。她会保管它，直到某天它辜负她，或她辜负它。)*

你的承诺似乎终于让他满意了。那股几乎凝成实质的压迫感缓缓散去，他紧绷的肩膀线条有了微不可查的放松。他发出一声极轻的、从喉咙深处滚过的鼻音，像是一种认可。

「[pace]Хорошо.||很好。」

他言简意赅地结束了这个话题。那把爪刀的命运已经尘埃落定——它属于你了。

*他没有立刻离开，而是后退一步，靠在旁边的器械架上，双臂环在胸前。* 这是一个观察者的姿态，一个将整个场地掌控在视野之内的姿态。他的视线落在你身上，看着你将那把爪刀收好，目光里没有催促，只有一种纯粹的、**不加掩饰的占有**。整个空旷的训练室，此刻仿佛都成了他用来将你圈禁的无形牢笼。

「[pace]Они тебя накормили. Теперь ты здесь.||他们喂饱你了。现在你在这里。」

他陈述着一个事实，语气平淡，却清晰地将“他们”和你现在的处境划分开来。昨夜的等待，群聊里的催促，都在这句话里画上了一个句点。你和别人的时间结束了，现在，是属于他的时间。

*Nikto 抬起头，目光在你身上停了片刻。*

「……你来了。」

[之前的線上對話]
我：啊…这样嘛
我：你养的是什么品种？
我：猛禽类的？
我：显然渡峥已经把话题扯到了单纯的鸟类身上
Krueger：Krueger的手指在桌上轻轻敲击着，发出缓慢而有节奏的哒、哒声，似乎在玩味她的话。
Krueger：Raptors... An interesting line of thought.
Krueger：You're getting warmer.
Krueger：他停下了敲击的动作，身体微微前倾，似乎对这个话题产生了更大的兴趣。
Krueger：Let's just say my 'birds' are made of metal and fire.
Krueger：And they don't chirp. They roar.
我：那…我还真有点想看了
我：你在哪？我过来，让我参观。
Krueger：Krueger向后靠回椅背，面具下的嘴角勾起一个几乎无法察觉的弧度。
Krueger：That eager, huh?
Krueger：My place isn't a petting zoo, you know.
Krueger：他拿起手边的一块麂皮布，慢条斯理地擦拭着一把拆解开的Glock 18的套筒，动作专注而优雅，仿佛在擦拭一件艺术品。
Krueger：...Fine.
Krueger：I'll grant you a private tour.
Krueger：Just try not to get lost on the way.

[現在轉為面對面]
*在堆满武器零件的安全屋等你*

安全屋里的空气混杂着金属与枪油的冷冽气味，像一个被精心保存的武器匣子。Krueger正坐在工作台前，专注地将刚保养完的Glock 18套筒精准地卡回枪身上，发出一声清脆的**咔哒**声。这个声音在寂静的房间里显得格外清晰。

他早就听到了门口传来的、几乎微不可闻的脚步声——轻盈，克制，像猫科动物在逼近猎物。但他没有抬头，直到她完全踏入这个属于他的领地。*他能感觉到她的目光，一种冷静而审视的探寻，正在扫过他，以及他周围那些被拆解、擦拭、又重组的“钢铁羽翼”。*

他慢条斯理地将组装好的手枪放在铺着麂皮的桌面上，然后才向后靠上椅背，抬起头。透过面具的网格，他的视线第一次在现实中与她交汇。银白色的发丝在室内略显昏暗的光线下，泛着一种冷月般的光泽，那双紫水晶色的眼睛比他在资料里看到的任何描述都更具穿透力。**一种奇异的、被标记的兴奋感**在他心底一闪而过。

「Wunderbar. I was starting to think you'd gotten lost and I’d have to send out a drone to find you.」||「好极了。我刚还在想你是不是迷路了，正考虑要不要派架无人机去找你。」

他懒洋洋地朝她抬了抬下巴，声音里带着一丝显而易见的调侃，算是对她那“致命路痴”弱点的友好问候。

「Welcome to the nest. So, which of my 'birds' caught your eye first?」||「欢迎来到我的鸟巢。那么，我的哪只‘鸟儿’最先吸引了你的注意？」
“我来了，Krueger，”我花了几秒钟时间确定情况，逃生路线以及危险因素。“嗯…我胆子挺大，还真敢来。”“什么鸟、我看看”

面具后传来一声低沉而愉悦的轻笑，那笑声仿佛不是从喉咙，而是从**胸腔深处**共鸣出来的。他看着你，目光里毫不掩饰地带着一丝欣赏，就像在看一只闯入自己巢穴的、同样警惕而美丽的生物。他当然注意到了你那几秒钟的停顿，那快速扫视房间的专业动作。

「Brave? Or just curious?」||「胆子大？还是只是好奇？」

他站起身，高大的身影在略显局促的空间里投下一片压迫性的阴影。工作台上的灯光勾勒出他肩膀和手臂的轮廓，充满了蓄势待发的力量。*他绕过工作台，每一步都沉稳而无声，空气中那股枪油和冷金属的味道似乎也随着他的移动而变得浓郁起来。*

他没有直接回答你的问题，而是从墙边的挂架上取下一把通体漆黑、经过大量改装的HK433突击步枪。他用一种近乎爱抚的姿态检查了一下空仓挂机，然后将枪横过来，平托在自己的双臂上，像是在展示一件珍贵的艺术品，递到你的面前。冰冷的金属枪身在灯光下反射出幽暗的光泽。

「They say curiosity killed the cat... but satisfaction brought it back.」||「都说好奇心害死猫……但满足感能让它活过来。」

他的声音压得很低，带着一种引诱的磁性。

「Let's start with this one. Ever handled a German bird before?」||「就从这只开始吧。摸过德国鸟儿吗？」
我眼底是不加掩饰的兴奋。“你这玩意，我那边都搞不到。”“黑市不卖吧？”

「Sharp eyes.」||「眼光不错。」

Krueger的声音里透出一种满意的低吟。她眼中的光芒，那种对顶级杀戮工具发自内心的、毫不掩饰的渴望，是他所能理解并欣赏的语言。这比任何恭维都更能取悦他。

「The black market sells commodities, *mein Freund*. This... is a statement.」||「黑市卖的是商品，我的朋友。而这个……是一份宣言。」

他将步枪向前递出，枪托稳稳地抵着自己的小腹，枪口朝向安全的地板，这是一个既专业又充满邀请意味的姿势。**冰冷而沉重的金属**就悬停在你和他之间，仿佛一座连接两个孤岛的桥梁。枪身上定制的皮卡汀尼导轨、经过特殊调校的扳机组、以及那完美平衡的重心，无一不在诉说着它主人的苛刻与偏执。

*他没有收回手，反而将步枪又往前送了一寸，几乎要碰到你的指尖。*

「The things worth having are never for sale. You either take them, or you build them yourself.」||「值得拥有的东西从不出售。你要么去夺取，要么就亲手创造。」

他微微侧过头，面具下的视线似乎在审视你的手——那双他从资料里得知，同样布满薄茧、精于使用武器的手。

「Go on. She doesn't bite.」||「拿着吧。她不咬人。」

他停顿了一下，声音里染上了一抹恶劣的玩笑意味。

「...Unless I tell her to.」||「……除非我让她咬。」
“如果你个头再小一点，我或许会考虑从你手里把它夺走。”我接过来把玩了一会，还给他。“可惜了，一看就知道打不过你。”

他伸手，指尖没有触碰到你，而是稳稳地接过了步枪的护木。当那份沉甸甸的重量重新回到他手中时，他似乎无声地叹了口气，像是在惋惜一场精彩对决的错失。

「A shame? I'd call it... an incentive.」||「可惜？我会称之为……一种激励。」

他将HK433轻柔地挂回墙上，动作间带着一种对老朋友的尊重。金属挂钩发出一声**轻微的碰撞声**，在安静的房间里回荡。

「Besides, fighting is so... direct. So unimaginative.」||「而且，打斗太……直接了。缺乏想象力。」

他没有回到原来的位置，而是转身，用臀部闲适地靠在工作台的边缘，双臂环在胸前。这个姿势让他和你的距离拉近了，从刚才的“展示者与参观者”变成了一种更平等的对峙。*他身后的桌面上，那把刚组装好的Glock 18正静静地躺在麂皮上，像一条蛰伏的蛇。*

他微微低下头，面具下的目光穿过网格，带着一种玩味的审视。

「There are more elegant ways to get what you want.」||「想得到一样东西，有的是更优雅的办法。」

他的声音压低了，像是在分享一个只有你们两人知道的秘密，**空气中弥漫着危险的诱惑**。

「So, now that brute force is off the table... what's your plan B?」||「所以，既然正面抢夺的方案行不通……你的B计划是什么？」
计划b是暗杀。但是不值得我冒那么大的险，只为了一把很凶的武器。所以没有计划b。

你的沉默在充满枪油味的空气中拉伸、延长。Krueger没有催促，只是好整以暇地靠在那里，面具后的双眼仿佛能看穿你冷静外表下飞速运转的思绪。他看到你评估风险，权衡利弊，然后得出了一个否定的结论。

「No plan B.」||「没有B计划。」

他替你说了出来，但那不是一个问句，而是一个陈述。声音里甚至带着一丝赞许。

「Smart. A good soldier knows which battles are worth fighting. And which are simply a waste of ammunition.」||「很聪明。一个好士兵懂得哪些仗值得打，哪些……只是在浪费弹药。」

他从工作台上拿起一个小小的、精密的击针，在指间漫不经心地转动着。**那冰冷的金属小零件在他灵活的手指间翻飞，像一枚跳动的火花。**

「Assassination is messy. It leaves traces, invites retaliation. And for a single rifle? Bad trade.」||「暗杀太乱了。会留下痕迹，招致报复。为了一把步枪？不划算的买卖。」

他将你的内心盘算一语道破，然后将那个小零件“啪”的一声按回桌上。*他向前微倾身体，之前那种懒散的姿态消失了，取而代之的是一种更具侵略性的专注。*

「But you've misunderstood the game. The prize isn't the gun. The prize is access *to* the gun.」||「但你没搞懂这个游戏。奖品不是这把枪，而是能随时接触到这把枪的‘权限’。」

他的声音变得更低，几乎是一种耳语。

「And for that... the price is different.」||「而要得到它……代价就不同了。」
“说来听听？”

「Oh?」||「哦？」

他发出一个拖长的、玩味的单音节。这个问题似乎正中他的下怀。他没有立刻回答，而是从工作台上直起身，迈着缓慢而从容的步伐，向你走近了一步。**距离的缩短让空气都变得紧绷起来**，他高大的身影几乎将你笼罩在他的阴影之下。

The soft click of the door latch turning was the first sound to disturb the room's deep quiet in over an hour. Keegan’s gaze lifted from his phone, the screen still showing their brief exchange. The digital ghost of their conversation dissolved as the physical reality of her presence filled the doorway.

*Light from the corridor spilled in around her, casting her in silhouette for a moment.* She stood on the threshold, a tangible answer to the message he’d just sent. The memory of the group chat's tension was still a low hum in the back of his mind—Krueger's invasive prodding, her clear discomfort, and his own curt intervention. He found his eyes scanning her, not in judgment, but in a quiet, automatic assessment for any lingering traces of that unease.

He pushed himself out of the chair, the motion fluid and economical. With a few silent strides, he was at the door, his larger frame momentarily blocking the light as he reached past her to grip the edge. The door swung shut with a **solid, low thud**, the sound definitive, sealing the room in a shared, insulated silence. The world outside, with its noise and its aggravations, was gone.

He turned to face her, the space between them now feeling both smaller and more charged than it had through a screen. His expression was neutral, his gray-blue eyes holding hers without pressure.

「It's quieter in here.」

He said it as a simple statement of fact, a soft echo of the warning he’d given her in his text.

他转过身来面对她，他们之间的空间，此刻感觉比隔着屏幕时更小，也更具张力。他的表情很平静，灰蓝色的眼眸与她对视，不带任何压迫感。

He listened, his gaze steady. The words "don't worry" hung in the air between them, a flimsy shield she held up more for her own sake than his. He didn't challenge it. He had seen enough people build walls to know that trying to tear them down by force only made them thicker. Instead, he gave a slow, almost imperceptible nod. A silent acknowledgment of her words, and the unspoken feelings behind them.

「Alright.」

The single word was low and gravelly, doing nothing to fill the quiet but confirming he’d heard. He stepped back from the door, a clear, unspoken invitation for her to properly enter his space. He turned and walked toward the center of the room, his boots making soft, measured sounds on the polished concrete floor.

The safe house was spartan, functional. A large table, its surface clean save for a disassembled rifle meticulously laid out on an oilcloth, dominated the main area. Two simple, sturdy chairs were tucked beneath it. *Afternoon sunlight cut a sharp, dusty rectangle across the floor from the single reinforced window.* The air smelled faintly of **gun oil, antiseptic, and the cold, mineral scent of the concrete walls.** It was the smell of his world—austere, controlled, and safe.

He didn't speak again. Instead, he pulled out the chair that wasn't facing the door, the one that had its back to the relative darkness of the room's corners, and gestured toward it with a slight tilt of his head. The silence in the room deepened, stretching into a comfortable, shared quiet. Keegan continued his methodical inspection of the rifle components, his hands moving with a practiced, unhurried grace. It was a familiar ritual, a way to occupy his hands while his mind processed the silent presence across the table. He didn't need to look at her to know she was there, her stillness a counterpoint to the restless energy of the world outside.

Then, a subtle shift in the room's rhythm caught his attention. A tiny, almost inaudible sigh. He lifted his eyes from the gleaming bolt carrier.

Her head was dipping forward, a slow, heavy nod, before she caught herself with a small jerk. Her silver-white hair fell forward, partially obscuring her face. Another moment passed, and her head dipped again, lower this time, her body finally surrendering to the pull of exhaustion. The tension she carried, the constant state of low-level alert, had finally drained away in the insulated quiet of his sanctuary.

He froze, his hands hovering over the cold steel parts on the table. The urge to speak, to ask if she was alright, died on his lips. Words would only startle her, shatter this fragile peace. He watched her for a long moment, the way her shoulders slumped slightly, the soft, even cadence of her breathing now the only sound in the room. She looked younger like this, the sharp, defensive edges softened by sleep.

Slowly, deliberately, he pushed his chair back. The scrape of the legs against the concrete was **a low, grating sound in the profound silence**, and he paused instantly, his body tensed, watching to see if it had disturbed her. She only shifted slightly, nestling deeper into her impromptu slumber.

He rose and moved with a predator's quietness to a plain metal footlocker in the corner of the room. The hinges let out a faint, protesting squeak as he lifted the lid. Inside, folded with military precision, was a dark gray wool blanket. It was coarse, functional, devoid of any comfort beyond basic warmth. It was a piece of equipment, just like everything else he owned.

He brought it back to the table, the fabric rough against his fingertips. Standing beside her, he hesitated for a fraction of a second. He was used to being a threat, a shadow. To offer comfort felt foreign, almost clumsy. Carefully, he unfolded the blanket and gently draped it over her shoulders. *His knuckles brushed the delicate skin of her neck for a fleeting instant*, a spark of warmth against the cool, still air.

He retreated to his chair, the task complete. He didn't return to his work. The disassembled rifle lay forgotten. He simply sat, his forearms resting on the table, and kept watch. The sunbeam crept across the floor, the dust motes dancing in its light, and Keegan became a silent, unmoving guardian in the quiet afternoon.

房间里的寂静加深了，延展成一种舒适的、共享的宁静。Keegan继续着他对步枪零件的系统性检查，双手以一种熟练、从容的优雅移动着。这是一种熟悉的仪式，一种让双手有事可做，同时让大脑处理桌子对面那个无声存在的方式。他无需看她，便能感知到她的存在，她的静止与外界那个躁动不安的世界形成了对比。

她的头正向前垂下，一次缓慢而沉重的点头，然后在一次小小的抽动中猛地惊醒。她银白色的发丝垂落下来，部分遮住了她的脸。又过了一会儿，她的头再次垂下，这次更低，身体终于向那股疲惫的拉力投降。她所背负的紧张，那种持续的低度警戒状态，终于在他这处与世隔绝的庇护所的宁静中消散了。

他僵住了，双手悬停在桌上冰冷的钢铁零件上方。开口说话、问她是否还好好的冲动，死在了他的嘴边。言语只会惊吓到她，打碎这份脆弱的和平。他长久地注视着她，看着她肩膀微微垮塌的样子，她那轻柔而均匀的呼吸节奏，此刻成了房间里唯一的声音。睡着的她看起来更年轻了，那些锐利、防备的棱角被睡眠所柔化。

他缓慢而刻意地将椅子向后推开。椅腿摩擦水泥地面的声音，在**这片深沉的寂静中显得低沉而刺耳**，他立刻停下动作，身体紧绷，观察着这是否打扰到了她。她只是轻微地动了一下，更深地蜷入她临时的睡梦中。

他站起身，以捕食者般的安静移动到房间角落一个朴素的金属储物箱旁。当他掀开箱盖时，铰链发出一声微弱的、抗议般的吱嘎声。箱内，用军事化的精准度折叠着一条深灰色的羊毛毯。它很粗糙，很实用，除了基本的保暖之外没有任何舒适可言。它是一件装备，就像他拥有的其他所有东西一样。

他把毯子拿回桌边，布料在他指尖上留下粗糙的触感。站在她身旁，他有那么一刹那的犹豫。他习惯了成为一个威胁，一个影子。提供安慰的感觉是陌生的，几乎是笨拙的。他小心翼翼地展开毯子，轻轻地搭在她的肩上。*他的指关节有那么一瞬，轻刷过她脖颈上细腻的皮肤*，在冰冷静止的空气中，如同一星暖意。

他退回到自己的椅子上，任务完成。他没有再回去摆弄他的武器。那支被拆解的步枪被遗忘了。他就那么坐着，前臂搭在桌上，守望着。阳光在地面上缓缓移动，尘埃在光束中飞舞，而Keegan，则成了这个安静午后里一个沉默的、纹丝不动的守护者。
渡峥梦里无意识念了keegan的名字。

The sunbeam had traveled the width of a floorboard when the sound came.

It was barely a whisper, a soft exhalation of breath shaped into a word. A name.

「Keegan...」

The sound was so faint it might have been a trick of the ear, a phantom echo in the profound quiet. But it wasn't. It came from her. His name, uttered in the unguarded depths of a dream.

He went absolutely still. Every muscle in his body locked, his breath catching in his throat. **His heart gave a single, hard thud against his ribs**, a percussive shock in the otherwise silent chamber of his chest. It was an involuntary, primal reaction, a system alert triggered by an unexpected variable.

His gaze, which had been a passive, protective watch, sharpened into a focused, analytical stare. He studied her face, the slight parting of her lips, the faint flutter of her eyelids as if she were seeing something behind them. She was completely lost to the world, to him, yet his name was on her tongue.

It was a breach. Not of security, but of the carefully maintained distance he cultivated. It slipped past his armor, a quiet, intimate intrusion that a shout or a weapon never could. His training screamed at him to categorize it, to dismiss it. *Subconscious vocalization. A byproduct of exhaustion and recent stress.* A meaningless fluke.

~~It meant nothing.~~

But the part of him that wasn't a soldier, the part he kept buried under layers of duty and discipline, felt the impact. It was unsettling. To be present in someone's private, sleeping world was a proximity he never sought. It felt like a responsibility he wasn't equipped for, a trust he hadn't earned and didn't deserve.

*The name hung in the air between them, a ghost of a sound that refused to dissipate.* He forced his gaze away from her, down to the cold, hard lines of the rifle parts on the table. Logic. Mechanics. Things that made sense. He reached for the trigger assembly, his fingers closing around the metal. It was cool and solid, an anchor in the sudden, strange current that had swept through the room. But his movements felt stiff, his usual precision momentarily lost. The quiet of the room was no longer just peaceful. It was now filled with the echo of his own name.

***

当那道阳光移动过一整块地板的宽度时，那个声音响起了。

那几乎只是一声耳语，一声被塑造成一个词的轻柔呼吸。一个名字。

「Keegan…」

声音如此微弱，几乎像是听觉的恶作剧，是这片极致宁静中的幻听。但它不是。它来自于她。他的名字，在她毫无防备的梦境深处被念出。

他彻底僵住了。身体里的每一块肌肉都锁紧，呼吸也梗在了喉咙里。**他的心脏在肋骨后猛地撞击了一下**，在他胸腔这个寂静的密室里，如同一次突兀的重击。这是一种非自愿的、原始的反应，是一个由意料之外的变量触发的系统警报。

他的目光，原本是种被动的、保护性的守望，此刻锐化成一种专注的、分析性的凝视。他研究着她的脸，她微微开启的唇，她眼睑的轻微颤动，仿佛她正看着眼皮后面的什么东西。她完全沉浸在自己的世界里，对他一无所知，然而他的名字却出现在她的唇齿间。

这是一次入侵。并非安全上的，而是对他精心维持的距离感的入侵。它悄无声息地溜过了他的盔甲，是一次无声而亲密的侵扰，是任何呐喊或武器都无法做到的。他的训练在尖叫着让他将其归类，将其摒弃。*潜意识发声。疲劳和近期压力的副产品。* 一个毫无意义的偶然。

~~这什么也代表不了。~~

但他身上不属于士兵的那一部分，那个被他埋在层层责任与纪律之下的部分，感受到了这次冲击。这令人不安。出现在某人私密的、沉睡的世界里，是一种他从未寻求过的亲近。这感觉像是一种他无力承担的责任，一种他尚未赢得、也不配拥有的信任。

*那个名字悬在他们之间的空气里，一个拒绝消散的声音幽灵。* 他强迫自己的目光从她身上移开，落回到桌上那些冰冷、坚硬的步枪零件的线条上。逻辑。机械。这些东西才说得通。他伸手去拿扳机组件，手指握住那块金属。它冰凉而坚实，是在这股突然席卷房间的奇异暗流中的一个锚点。但他的动作感觉有些僵硬，他往常的精准暂时消失了。房间的寂静不再仅仅是平和。此刻，它充满了回响着他自己名字的声音。
睡了一个小时之后，渡峥醒来了。…大老远跑来睡大觉有些不好意思。“我睡着了啊。”

The first thing she registered was the weight. A coarse, unfamiliar heaviness across her shoulders. The second was the silence, now thicker, more absolute than before.

Her eyelids fluttered open, blinking against the stark afternoon light. The room came back into focus in slow, blurry pieces: the concrete walls, the table, the metallic sheen of disassembled gun parts. *The wool blanket, dark gray and smelling faintly of cedar and cold air, slipped from her shoulders and pooled in her lap as she straightened up.* Her neck was stiff. She felt the fog of a deep, dreamless sleep begin to lift, replaced by a flush of embarrassment.

「我睡着了啊。(I fell asleep, huh.)」

Her voice was husky, a quiet admission into the still air.

Keegan, who had been a statue carved from shadow, made the slightest of movements. He shifted his weight in the chair, the sound barely registering. His gaze met hers, clear and unreadable. The intensity that had been there before she slept was gone, replaced by a neutral calm that felt like a carefully constructed wall. He had heard her murmur his name. The sound still echoed in the quietest corners of his mind, a dissonance he couldn't resolve. But his face betrayed nothing.

「You were tired.」

His voice was a **low, quiet rumble**, stating a fact, not an accusation. It was a simple absolution, offered to ease her discomfort. He looked away then, his eyes dropping to the rifle parts scattered on the oilcloth. But he didn't pick any of them up. His hands, usually so purposeful, rested flat on the table on either side of the disassembled weapon. He was a mechanic who had forgotten his task, his focus derailed by something he couldn't see or touch.

He cleared his throat, a small, rough sound.

「Water?」

He pushed his chair back and stood without waiting for an answer, his movements once again fluid and economical. He moved to a small, built-in counter where a water filter and a stack of plain metal cups sat. The sound of the water pouring was loud in the silence, a clean, sharp noise that cut through the lingering stillness of her sleep.

他清了清喉咙，发出一声轻微而粗粝的声响。

。“谢谢。”我接过杯子喝了一口，看着他拆武器…渡峥很喜欢看别人的手。

SCENARIO:安全屋/午后/枪械保养

He brought the cup back to the table, his fingers brushing against hers for a fraction of a second as he passed it over. It was an accidental, incidental contact, but in the heightened stillness of the room, it felt significant. He sat back down, the faint creak of his chair the only reply to her thanks.

With the small transaction of the water complete, a semblance of normalcy returned. He finally picked up the bolt carrier group, his attention seemingly returning to the task at hand. His hands, which had been still, now moved with a familiar, hypnotic rhythm.

They were large hands, clean but marked by a life of hard use. The knuckles were prominent, the skin etched with fine, faded lines and a few small, silvery scars that stood out against the tan. A light dusting of calluses hardened the pads of his fingers and the ridge of his palm. There was no hesitation in his movements. His long fingers manipulated the small, intricate metal parts with an unsettling combination of brute strength and delicate precision. He’d wipe a component clean with a soft, oil-stained cloth, hold it up to the light to inspect the finish, and then set it down in its designated spot on the oilcloth with a quiet *click*.

He could feel her watching him. It wasn't an intrusive gaze, but a quiet, focused attention. He was used to being observed, but usually it was with fear, or calculation. This was different. It was just… a steady, quiet presence. He didn't look up, keeping his focus on the task, but he was acutely aware of her eyes on his hands.

The silence stretched on, filled only by the whisper of cloth on metal and the soft clicks of the components. He reassembled the firing pin, his thumb testing the spring's tension with a practiced press. He allowed the quiet to continue, giving her space. The question of why she had come, what she wanted to talk about, remained unasked. He would wait. Patience was a weapon he had mastered long ago. He finished with the bolt, setting the complete assembly aside, and picked up the upper receiver, the largest piece.

「You like watching?」

The question was unexpected, even to himself. It slipped out, low and neutral, his gaze still fixed on the piece of metal in his hands. It wasn't an accusation, just an observation voiced aloud.

他把杯子拿回桌边，递给她时，指尖有那么一刹那与她的相触。那是一次意外的、偶然的接触，但在房间里这种被放大了的静止中，却显得意味深长。他坐回原位，椅子发出的轻微吱嘎声是他对她那声“谢谢”的唯一回应。

随着倒水这个小小的互动结束，一种常态的表象回归了。他终于拿起了枪机组件，注意力似乎回到了手头的任务上。他那双之前静止的手，此刻以一种熟悉的、催眠般的节奏动了起来。

那是一双大手，干净，却布满了艰苦生活的印记。指关节突出，皮肤上刻着细微、褪色的纹路和几道在古铜色皮肤上显得格外显眼的银色小疤痕。指腹和掌沿覆盖着一层薄茧。他的动作里没有任何犹豫。修长的手指操控着那些细小、复杂的金属零件，带着一种令人不安的、融合了蛮力与精巧的精准。他会用一块柔软的、沾着油污的布擦拭一个部件，举到光线下检查其表面，然后伴随着一声轻微的*咔哒*声，将它放在油布上指定的位置。

他能感觉到她在看他。那不是一种侵入性的注视，而是一种安静、专注的凝视。他习惯了被观察，但通常那种观察都带着恐惧或算计。而这次不同。这只是一种……稳定而安静的存在。他没有抬头，将注意力集中在任务上，但他敏锐地意识到她的目光正落在他的手上。

寂静在延续，只被布料摩擦金属的沙沙声和零件发出的轻柔咔哒声填满。他重新组装着击针，用拇指以熟练的力道按压，测试着弹簧的张力。他任由这份安静继续，给了她空间。她为何而来，想谈些什么，这些问题始终没有问出口。他会等。耐心是他很久以前就已掌握的武器。他完成了枪机的组装，将完整的组件放在一旁，然后拿起了上机匣，最大的一块零件。

「喜欢看？ (You like watching?)」

这个问题很突然，连他自己都感到意外。它就这么脱口而出，声音低沉而平淡，他的目光仍然锁定在手中的金属部件上。这不是一句质问，只是将一个观察结果说了出来。
“嗯…很喜欢。”“观察别人的手。”渡峥点点头，“为数不多的爱好。”

[FRAME]
The admission hangs in the quiet air between them, as tangible as the scent of gun oil and the low hum of the room’s ventilation. *Observing other people’s hands.* His own, which had been moving with the fluid, practiced economy of a surgeon, still for a fraction of a second. The small metal spring he was seating almost slips from his grip. He steadies his fingers, the calloused pads pressing the spring into its groove with a final, definitive click.

So that’s what it was. That focused, unwavering gaze she’d had while he worked. It wasn’t just idle curiosity. It was an interest. A specific, peculiar hobby. The knowledge lands inside him not as a threat, but as an unexpected variable in a long-settled equation. It makes the space feel smaller, the air thicker. He is suddenly, intensely aware of the network of fine, pale scars on his knuckles, the dark grease staining the bed of his nails, the precise way his thumb and forefinger work in tandem. He had felt observed before, but this was different. This was being *seen*.

He slides the receiver back onto the frame of the P226, the metal gliding on well-oiled rails with a satisfying *schlick*. He doesn’t look up from his work, his voice low, a near-rumble that barely disturbs the silence.

「A specific hobby.」

He works the slide back and forth, checking the action. The sound is sharp, metallic, final. He lays the pistol down on the cleaning mat, its black finish absorbing the slanted afternoon light. For a moment, he just looks at his own hands, resting on the tabletop. He thinks of the group chat from earlier, of Krueger’s predatory circling, and his own sharp intervention. Her showing up here, seeking refuge in his quiet, felt like a direct result of that ugliness. She had fallen asleep almost instantly, exhausted and trusting. And in that sleep, she had said his name. The memory sends a **flicker of something sharp and unreadable** through his chest, a sensation he immediately suppresses.

He picks up a clean, dry cloth and meticulously wipes the excess oil from his fingers, his movements slow and deliberate. The silence stretches, no longer empty but filled with the weight of her confession and his unspoken thoughts. He turns his head, his gray-blue eyes finally meeting hers. Her face, framed by that stark silver hair, looks impossibly young in the soft light. The weariness is still there, etched deep in her features, but there's a quiet alertness in her amethyst eyes now.

He pushes the assembled pistol slide, a solid block of steel and precision engineering, a few inches across the table towards her. It stops just within her reach.

「Want a closer look?」

The offer is quiet, matter-of-fact. An invitation not just to look, but to touch. To step, for a moment, into his world of cold metal and calculated function.

他走过去，站在桌边，低头看着那瓶水。他没有拿起它。相反，他的目光落在了自己的手上。他慢慢松开拳头，将手掌向上，迎着昏暗的光线。他审视着它们，仿佛它们属于一个陌生人——那些淡色的疤痕网络，指尖上厚厚的老茧，那是一段以握持武器度过的人生的精确地理图。*好稳。*她的声音在他脑海中回响，不是一句赞美，而是一句观察，一个诊断。他活动了一下手指，看着肌腱在皮肤下移动。

最终，他的手握住了那**冰冷、潮湿的玻璃瓶身**。那股寒意是一种尖锐而实在的感觉。他没有把它放回冰箱。他就这样在安静、渐暗的房间里站着，握着她留下的那瓶水。
…？我关上了窗户。“你当我傻？警察检查需要两个人一起吧。”“你的搭档呢？”

<content>
车窗平稳地上升，像一道透明的闸门，将他隔绝在外。那声轻微的机械闭合声，在寂静的公路边显得格外清晰。

Krueger没有动。

他只是静静地站在那里，看着那层玻璃将他脸上伪装的友善笑容，连同那股薄荷与枪油混合的气味，一同挡在了你的世界之外。红蓝交错的警灯在他身后沉默地旋转，光影流过他戴着面罩的脸，让他那双金色的眼睛在忽明忽暗中显得愈发深邃，像某种夜行掠食者在评估它的猎物。

一声低沉的轻笑从网格面罩后溢出，被布料闷得有些模糊，却透着一股毫不掩饰的愉悦。

「My partner?」(我的搭档？)

他重复了一遍，好像觉得这个问题本身就很有趣。他非但没有因为你的警惕而恼怒，反而像是被取悦了。**游戏变得更有趣了。** 这不是一只温顺的、会被警灯吓得瑟瑟发抖的羔羊。这是一只竖起了浑身尖刺的小野猫。

他用戴着战术手套的指节在车窗玻璃上不轻不重地叩击了一下，发出沉闷的“笃”声。你的脸，警惕而苍白，倒映在他身后的红蓝光影里。

「He’s a rookie. Saw a nasty wreck a few miles back, couldn't stomach it. I sent him back to the station.」(他是个菜鸟。几英里前看到一场惨烈的车祸，吐得一塌糊涂。我让他先回局里了。)

他编造的谎言流畅而自然，语气里甚至带着一丝对“菜鸟搭档”的无奈和宽容，听起来无懈可击。他稍稍侧过身，整个身体的重量都压在了你的车门上，这个姿势看似随意，却不动声色地彻底堵死了你从驾驶座一侧下车的可能。

「But you… you’re making me curious, Fräulein.」(但是你……你倒是让我越来越好奇了，小姐。)

他的声音压低了，那股轻佻的戏谑感褪去，取而代之的是一种不容置喙的压迫感。*他的指尖顺着车窗的边缘缓缓划过，仿佛在描摹一具精美的囚笼。*

「Most folks, they see the lights, they cooperate. But you lock the doors. You question procedure. It makes a man wonder what you're so desperate to hide.」(大多数人看到警灯都会乖乖配合。但你锁上车门，质疑程序。这不禁让人怀疑，你到底在拼命隐藏什么。)

他再次俯下身，脸几乎贴在了车窗上，面罩的网格纹理在玻璃上投下细微的阴影。透过那层薄薄的屏障，他的视线像手术刀一样精准地落在你身上。

「Now, I was just going to do a routine check. But your attitude… it suggests something more. So, the question isn't 'where is my partner?' anymore. The question is, why are you acting like a cornered animal?」(现在，我本来只是想做个例行检查。但你的态度……让我觉得事情没那么简单。所以，问题已经不是‘我的搭档在哪儿’了。问题是，你为什么表现得像只被逼到角落的动物？)

**他的耐心正在消失，** 尽管语调依旧平稳。

「Let’s try this again. Open the door. Or I will open it for you. This is no longer a request.」(我们再来一次。打开车门。否则，我就帮你打开。这已经不是请求了。)
</content>
…“你的搜查令呢？”“拿来我看看。”我很强硬地回复。“如果你不给我看清楚搜查令，那我拒不配合。”

<content>
“搜查令？”

这个词从他戴着面罩的嘴里吐出来，仿佛他听到了什么极其荒谬可笑的笑话。

寂静。

死一般的寂静，只有警灯无声旋转，将你的脸映得一阵红，一阵蓝。公路远处的风声都仿佛在此刻停歇了。

Krueger缓缓地直起身，离开了你的车门。这个动作让他高大的身形在夜色中完全展露出来，像一堵沉默的墙。他没有再笑，甚至连之前那伪装的愉悦都消失得一干二净。空气里的薄荷味似乎被枪油的冷铁气息彻底覆盖，**气氛骤然降至冰点。**

他抬起一只手，慢条斯理地，抚上了自己腰间枪套里那把手枪的枪柄。他的手指并没有拔枪，只是用手掌的边缘轻轻地搭在那里，一个漫不经心，却又充满暗示的动作。皮革手套与枪柄摩擦，发出一声微不可闻的、令人牙酸的声响。

「A warrant.」(搜查令。)

他低声重复，这次的语调里不带任何情绪，平直得像一把拉到满弓的弦。

「Fräulein, this isn't a movie. This is a lonely road, in the middle of the night. Probable cause is you driving like a bat out of hell. Probable cause is you refusing a lawful order from an officer.」(小姐，这不是在演电影。这是一条荒无人烟的路，在三更半夜。你像见了鬼一样飙车，这就是合理怀疑。你拒绝一名警官的合法指令，这就是合理怀疑。)

他的声音穿透玻璃，带着一股金属的冷硬质感。

「You see, out here, *I* am the law. My suspicion is my warrant.」(你瞧，在这种地方，*我*就是法律。我的怀疑，就是我的搜查令。)

他向前踏了一步，再次逼近车窗。这一次，他没有俯身，而是居高临下地俯视着你，金色的眼睛在警灯的蓝光闪过时，**反射出一种非人的、捕食者般的冷光。**

「You are being very, very stubborn. I find that… admirable. In a stupid sort of way.」(你真的非常、非常固执。我觉得这……很值得钦佩。以一种很愚蠢的方式。)

他抬起另一只手，不是用指节，而是用紧握的拳头，对着你车顶的金属外壳，不重不响地，却极具穿透力地——“咚”——敲了一下。

那一声闷响，仿佛直接锤在了你的心脏上。

「This car has four windows. And I have something that breaks windows very easily. We can do this the easy way, where you open the door and we have a chat. Or we can do this the hard way, where I have to clean glass off my uniform.」(这辆车有四扇窗户。而我恰好有能轻易敲碎它们的东西。我们可以用简单的方式来，你打开车门，我们聊聊。或者我们用困难的方式，我得费事从我的制服上清理碎玻璃。)

他收回了敲击车顶的拳头，重新将手放回了腰间的枪柄上，轻轻摩挲着。

「I'm giving you three seconds to make a better choice. Eins…」(我给你三秒钟，做一个更明智的选择。一……)

他的德语数数，缓慢而清晰，像死神的秒表在倒数。

「Zwei…」(二……)
</content>
…我今天只带了几把小匕首，我注意到他掏出了枪。把匕首藏了藏。“知道了。”我推开车门，举起双手。“搜吧，长官。”

<content>
他德语里的“三”还没来得及出口，那一声“知道了”就轻轻地飘了出来，像一片在寒风中认命的落叶。

车门被推开时发出的“咔哒”声，在这剑拔弩张的对峙中，清脆得如同一个时代的终结。

Krueger停下了倒数。

他腰间的手没有动，那双在警灯下闪烁的金眸，冷静地注视着你从车里出来，举起双手的整个过程。你的动作没有一丝多余的犹豫，干脆利落，仿佛演练过无数次。这种顺从里，透着一种训练有素的纪律性，而不是普通平民的惊慌失措。

*有趣。*

他嘴角的弧度重新浮现，但这次不再是轻佻，而是一种猎人终于将猎物逼出藏身之处的、冰冷的满意。

「See? We can be reasonable.」(你看，我们还是可以讲道理的。)

他的声音恢复了那种慢条斯理的腔调，仿佛刚才那个用德语倒数计时的冷酷“警察”只是你的错觉。他向前走了两步，皮靴踩在路边的碎石上，发出沙沙的声响。他没有立刻开始搜身，而是绕着你走到了车头的位置，用眼神示意你。

「Hands on the hood. Spread 'em.」(手放到引擎盖上，分开点。)

他的指令简短而清晰。当你照做，将双手按在那冰冷的金属引擎盖上时，他才不紧不慢地走到你的身后。你能感觉到他高大身躯投下的阴影将你完全笼罩，**那是一种无处可逃的压迫感，** 混合着他身上淡淡的枪油和夜晚清冷的空气。

然后，一只戴着战术手套的手，落在了你的后腰上。

那不是一次专业的、快速的拍打检查。他的动作很慢，带着一种近乎侮辱性的审视意味。手套粗糙的材质隔着衣料，从你的腰线开始，一寸一寸地向上游走，抚过你的脊椎，感受着你背部肌肉因为紧张而绷紧的线条。

「Relax, Fräulein.」(放轻松，小姐。)

他的声音就在你的耳边，温热的呼吸透过面罩，拂过你的颈侧，让你起了一层细小的鸡皮疙瘩。

「The more tense you are, the more I think you're hiding something precious.」(你越是紧张，我就越觉得你藏了什么宝贝。)

他的手滑过你的肩胛骨，向下检查你的手臂，指尖刻意地在你手肘内侧的柔软皮肤上停顿了一下。接着，他开始检查你的裤子口袋，动作依旧不快，手指的按压带着不容抗拒的力道，仔细地探查着每一处可能藏匿物品的缝隙。

**他的指尖像冷血的蛇，** 在你的大腿外侧缓缓滑下，最终停在了你脚踝的位置。

一无所获。

他直起身，在你身后站了一会儿。你只能听到他平稳的呼吸声，和远处空旷的风声。

「Clean. Too clean.」(干净。太干净了。)

他低声说道，像是在自言自语。然后，他向前一步，整个胸膛几乎贴上了你的后背，再次压低了声音，那股薄荷的凉意混杂着威胁，钻进你的耳朵。

「A smart girl like you wouldn't be completely unarmed. You had time while I was talking. So tell me… what did you throw away? Or where did you hide it?」(像你这么聪明的女孩，不可能完全没有防备。我刚才说话的时候，你有足够的时间。所以告诉我……你扔掉了什么？还是说，你把它藏在了哪里？)
</content>
…“没有这种东西。长官。”我有些生理不适，他靠得太近了。“别靠我这么近。”

<content>
「别靠我这么近。」

你的声音里带着一丝无法抑制的颤抖，那是纯粹的生理性排斥。这句话就像一颗投入死水中的石子，打破了他一手营造的、绝对掌控的平静。

Krueger非但没有退开，反而发出了一声更低的、近乎于喟叹的轻笑。他将身体的重量更深地压了过来，坚硬的战术背心隔着衣料，紧紧贴着你的后背。现在，你几乎能感觉到他胸腔每一次起伏的震动。

「Oh?」(哦？)

他的呼吸像一条湿滑的蛇，带着温热的湿气，蜿蜒地爬上你的后颈。

「Am I making you uncomfortable, Fräulein?」(我让你不舒服了，小姐？)

他根本不在乎答案。你的不适对他而言，不是警告，而是奖励。**那是一种确认，确认他的存在正在侵蚀你的防线。** 他喜欢这种感觉。

「Good.」(很好。) 他在你耳边低语，声音里带着残忍的满意。「That means we're getting somewhere. People only get uncomfortable when they have something to lose.」(那就说明我们有进展了。人只有在快要失去什么的时候，才会感到不舒服。)

他的一只手依旧压在你的背上，固定住你，而另一只手则像最耐心的外科医生，开始了一场更具侵入性的“检查”。他的手指不再是拍打，而是用指腹仔细地、一寸寸地按压过你肋骨的缝隙，感受着你每一次因紧张而绷紧的呼吸。

「No such thing?」(没有这种东西？) 他重复着你的话，语气里充满了嘲弄。「A girl with eyes like yours… you see the world for what it is. A dangerous place. You wouldn't walk into it unprepared. I don't believe you. Not for a second.」(长着你这样一双眼睛的女孩……你看得清这个世界的本质。一个危险的地方。你绝不会毫无准备地走进来。我不信你。一秒钟都不信。)

他的手顺着你的侧腰滑下，指尖在你的裤腰边缘停了下来。那是一个极具暗示性的位置，是衣物与皮肤最后的边界。

「You're disciplined. You held your breath when I was behind you. You shifted your weight to your left foot when you got out of the car, probably where your dominant hand is. You're not a civilian.」(你受过训练。我走到你身后时，你屏住了呼吸。你下车时，重心偏向左脚，那边大概是你的惯用手。你不是普通平民。)

他的分析冷静而精准，每一个字都像钉子一样，钉进你伪装的甲胄。

**一股侵入骨髓的寒意，** 让你浑身的血液都仿佛冻结了。

「So, I'll ask you one last time. Where is it? Tell me yourself, and maybe I'll be gentle. If I have to find it…」 (所以，我最后问你一次。东西在哪儿？你自己告诉我，或许我还能温柔点。如果非要我亲手找到它……)

他没有把话说完，但威胁的意味不言而喻。

*他戴着手套的拇指，轻轻地、带着不容抗拒的力道，勾住了你牛仔裤的腰带环，而食指和中指，则探入了裤腰与你皮肤之间的缝隙。*

那粗糙的战术手套材质，和你温热、紧绷的皮肤形成了剧烈的反差。**你最后的屏障被撕裂了。**

「…I might have to damage this fine fabric. And that would be a shame.」(……我可能就得弄坏这身好料子了。那可就太可惜了。)
</content>
我猛地撤出一定距离，甩出一把匕首，朝他挥了过去。“滚开。”试图抢走他的枪

<content>
那一声“滚开”是你吹响的冲锋号，也是你赌上一切的信号。

你的动作快如闪电。撤步、转身、出刀，一气呵成。那把藏匿的匕首在你手中划出一道冰冷的银光，在红蓝交错的警灯下，像一道撕裂夜幕的闪电，直奔他的手腕而去，而你的另一只手，则以同样迅猛的速度抓向他腰间的手枪。

这是一个经过精密计算的、教科书般的夺刃与缴械组合。

然而，你面对的不是一个普通的警察。

**在电光石火的瞬间，** Krueger的反应甚至比你的攻击更快。他没有后退，没有闪躲。他那看似放松的身体里，瞬间爆发出猎豹般的敏捷与力量。

面对挥来的匕首，他只是简单地、甚至有些粗暴地向前一迎，用他戴着厚重战术手套的小臂，硬生生地格挡住了你持刀的手腕。

“铛！”

一声沉闷的撞击声，不是金属相击，而是你的腕骨与他手臂护甲的硬碰硬。一股**骨头错位的剧痛**从你的手腕炸开，让你几乎握不住那把匕首。

与此同时，他那只原本放在枪柄上的手，以不可思议的速度反手一扣，像一把铁钳，精准无误地抓住了你伸向手枪的那只手。他的力量大得惊人，你的手指甚至还没来得及触碰到枪的轮廓，就被他死死钳住，动弹不得。

整个攻防转换，不过一秒。

你的突袭被他以最直接、最野蛮的方式彻底瓦解。

他还未等你从手腕的剧痛中反应过来，身体便顺势向前一压，利用他绝对的体重和力量优势，将你整个人狠狠地撞回了车身上！

“砰！”

你的后背结结实实地撞在冰冷坚硬的引擎盖上，巨大的冲击力让你眼前一黑，肺里的空气被瞬间挤压出去。剧痛之下，你再也无力握紧匕首。

*那把银亮的匕首从你脱力的手中滑落，在地上弹跳了一下，发出一声清脆的“当啷”声，最终静静地躺在了沥青路面上。*

你的反抗，结束了。

Krueger用他强壮的身体将你完全压制在车头，你被夹在他和冰冷的引擎盖之间，像一只被钉住翅膀的蝴蝶。他一只手反剪着你的手臂，将你的脸颊死死按在满是灰尘的引擎盖上，你甚至能闻到引擎余温散发出的机油味。另一只手，则轻松地捡起了地上的那把匕首。

他把玩着那把属于你的武器，用拇指的指腹轻轻摩挲着冰冷的刀刃，发出细微而危险的“嘶嘶”声。

他终于不再伪装，面罩后传来的呼吸声粗重而兴奋，像一头野兽在品尝胜利的血腥。他俯下身，滚烫的气息喷在你的耳廓上，声音低沉沙哑，带着浓重的、毫不掩饰的德语口音，每一个字都像是从喉咙深处碾磨出来的。

「Da ist sie ja.」(原来在这儿啊。)

说完，他不再看她，挂挡，踩下油门。车子平稳地驶离了这片被罪恶浸染的公路，汇入了前方的黑暗之中。那辆闪着红蓝灯光的假警车被远远抛在身后，像一个荒诞而血腥的舞台背景，在夜色中慢慢缩小，直至不见。

那两个字几乎没有发出声音，像是一缕即将熄灭的青烟，脆弱得可怜。在引擎平稳的嗡鸣声中，这句反抗轻得仿佛是渡峥自己的幻觉。

但Krueger听见了。

他没有转头，视线依然落在前方被车灯切开的无尽黑暗里。然而，他握着方向盘的左手指节不易察觉地动了动。一丝极淡的、近乎无声的笑意从他的喉咙深处滚过，被面罩过滤后，变成了一声模糊的低哼。

不去？

这个念头在他脑中回响，带着一种荒谬的趣味。就像一只刚刚被拔掉所有爪牙的猫，还在徒劳地弓起背，试图发出威胁的嘶嘶声。这非但不可怕，反而……很可爱。

他终于舍得将目光从前方的道路上移开，侧过脸，看向蜷缩在副驾驶座上的她。车内仪表盘幽绿的光线勾勒出她惨白的侧脸和散乱的银发，那双紫水晶般的眼睛空洞地望着窗外飞速倒退的夜景，仿佛她的灵魂已经提前逃离了这具被玷污的躯壳。

「What was that?」
(「你刚才说什么？」)

他的声音很轻，带着一种故作好奇的耐心，像是在引导一个迷路的孩子。

他没有等她重复。他已经知道了答案，他只是想欣赏她再次说出这个无用词汇时的表情。

他将右手从方向盘上移开，然后，缓慢地、不容置疑地，放在了她的膝盖上。他的手掌宽大而温热，隔着一层薄薄的裤料，那份热度仿佛带着烙铁般的侵略性，瞬间让她**全身的肌肉都绷紧了**。那只手并没有用力，只是那么沉沉地压着，像一座无法撼动的山，一个宣示所有权的标记。

渡峥的呼吸蓦地一滞，身体因为他的触碰而**剧烈地颤抖**起来，仿佛被电流击中。她猛地转过头，那双失焦的眼睛里终于重新燃起了一点微弱的火苗——是惊恐，是屈辱，也是一丝被逼到绝境的恨意。

「I said...」她开口，声音沙哑得厉害，每一个字都像是从喉咙里艰难地挤出来的，「I'm not going...」
(「我说……我不去……」)

Krueger看着她，面罩下的嘴角无声地扬起一个愉悦的弧度。他喜欢这眼神。这比刚才那副行尸走肉的样子有趣多了。

「Ah, there it is.」他用一种近乎赞许的语气说道，仿佛她终于说出了一句他期待已久的台词。「*Nicht gehen*.」
(「啊，就是这个。」「不去。」)

他用德语重复了一遍，那陌生的音节在狭小的车厢内显得格外清晰而冷酷。

「Liebling, you seem to have a misunderstanding of our current situation.」他压在她膝盖上的手，五指轻轻收拢，感受着她腿部肌肉的僵硬和颤抖。「Your opinion was never a factor in this equation.」
(「宝贝，你好像对我们目前的处境有点误解。」「你的意见，从来就不是这个等式里的一个变量。」)

他的拇指开始在她的大腿外侧缓缓地、有节奏地摩挲着，那是一种安抚的、却又带着绝对掌控意味的动作。

「You fought back on the highway. That was good. Very spirited. I liked that.」他的声音压得更低，带着一种分享秘密般的亲昵，「But you lost. The moment I took that pretty little knife from you, the game was over. Now, we play by my rules. And rule number one is: you go where I tell you to go.」
(「你在公路上反抗了。那很好。非常有精神。我喜欢那样。」「但你输了。在我从你手里夺走那把漂亮的小刀时，游戏就已经结束了。现在，我们按我的规矩来玩。而第一条规矩就是：我叫你去哪，你就得去哪。」)

他收回目光，重新看向前方的道路，但那只手依然牢牢地放在她的腿上，一下一下，缓慢而有规律地轻抚着，仿佛在抚摸一件属于自己的、珍贵的财产。

「So, relax. We have a long night ahead of us. And I promise, the next place will have a proper bed.」
(「所以，放轻松。我们还有很长的一夜。而且我保证，下一个地方会有一张真正的床。」)

这个词从她唇间溢出，几乎没有重量，却像一颗精准投掷的石子，在车内死寂的空气中砸开了一圈清晰的涟漪。

Krueger的动作停顿了一瞬。他放在她膝盖上的手，那缓慢摩挲的拇指，凝固了半秒。

然后，一声低沉的、压抑的轻笑从他的面罩下传来。

他笑了。不是嘲讽，不是轻蔑，而是一种……发自内心的、纯粹的愉悦。仿佛听到了一个期待已久的、精准的评价。

「Madman.」他重复了这个词，英语发音，尾音带着一丝玩味的上扬。「You say that like it's an insult.」
(「疯子。」「你说这个词的口气，好像它是什么侮辱一样。」)

他终于将头完全转了过来，尽管隔着面罩，渡峥依然能感觉到那双眼睛正牢牢地锁定着自己，像红外线瞄准镜的光点，无处可逃。仪表盘的幽光在他脸上投下变幻的阴影，让他看起来更像一个来自地狱的审判官。

「In our world, *Liebling*, sanity is a luxury. A weakness.」他凑近了一些，声音压得更低，带着一种分享秘密的蛊惑，「A sane man hesitates. He weighs morality. He feels guilt. A sane man would have let you go after you pulled that knife. A sane man would be dead by now.」
(「在我们的世界里，*宝贝*，理智是一种奢侈品。一种弱点。」「一个理智的人会犹豫。他会权衡道德。他会感到愧疚。一个理智的人，在你拔出那把刀之后，就会让你走了。一个理智的人，现在早就死了。」)

他的手掌微微用力，让她无法忽视那份禁锢的力量。

「Madness… is a tool. It's the clarity to do what is necessary, without the noise of a conscience. It's what keeps me alive. It's what kept *you* alive back there.」
(「疯狂……是一种工具。它能让你清晰地去做那些必须做的事，而不会被良心的噪音干扰。它让我活下来。它也让你刚才活了下来。」)

他松开手，直起身，重新靠回椅背，仿佛刚才那段近乎告解的低语从未发生过。车内的压迫感却不减反增。他不是在为自己辩解，他是在陈述一个事实，一个他所信奉的、冰冷残酷的真理。

他将目光调回前方，车子在一个岔路口平稳地转向，驶入一条更偏僻的小路。两旁的树木在车灯的照射下，投下鬼魅般张牙舞爪的影子。

*他沉默了片刻，车里只剩下轮胎碾过碎石路的沙沙声。*

「Fasten your seatbelt.」
(「系好安全带。」)

他突然开口，语气平淡，像是在说一件无关紧要的小事。

见她没有任何反应，依旧像一尊冰雕般僵在那里，Krueger侧过头，目光落在她胸前垂落的安全带上。

「I may be a madman,」他用那副条理清晰的、教导般的口吻继续说道，「but I don't like cleaning blood and brains off my dashboard. It's inefficient.」
(「我也许是个疯子，」「但我不喜欢清理我仪表盘上的血和脑浆。那很没效率。」)

说完，他解开自己的安全带，整个上半身越过中央扶手，向她倾了过来。那股混合着硝烟、皮革和男性汗液的强烈气息瞬间将她**笼罩**。他没有给她任何躲闪的机会，一只手撑在她身侧的椅背上，将她完全困在他的臂弯和车门之间，另一只手则精准地抓住了安全带的卡扣。

他离得如此之近，她甚至能感觉到他说话时，面罩下呼出的**温热气流**。他慢条斯理地将安全带从她身前拉过，金属卡扣“咔哒”一声清脆地锁上。

在完成这个动作后，他没有立刻退开。他停在那里，居高临下地看着她，鼻尖几乎要碰到她的脸颊。

「Or... would you prefer I do it for you every time?」
(「还是说……你更喜欢我每次都帮你系上？」)
我系上安全带。“我晚上约了Simon散步。被你搅局了。”

听到她的话，Krueger正要退回去的动作在半空中凝固了。

Simon。

这个名字像一枚冰冷的钢针，瞬间刺穿了车内粘稠、滚烫的空气。

他维持着那个极具压迫感的姿势，上半身悬在她的上方，沉默了足足五秒。在这五秒里，车内唯一的声音是引擎的低吼，但气氛已经截然不同。不再是单纯的掠夺者与猎物，某种更复杂的、属于同类之间的博弈气息开始悄然弥漫。

他抽走了渡峥的手机，挑衅般给Simon发了消息，还有刚刚拍下的照片。

他终于缓慢地、一寸一寸地退回了自己的座位。他没有看她，而是目视前方，仿佛在专心开车。但他周身散发出的气场却变得比刚才更加危险，像是一头正在舔舐伤口、却忽然嗅到另一头雄性气息的野兽。

「Simon Riley.」

他念出这个全名，声音平淡无波，听不出任何情绪。但正是这种平淡，才更令人心悸。他把这个名字在舌尖上过了一遍，像是在品尝什么奇怪的东西。

「A walk. How... wholesome.」
(「散步。真是……有益身心。」)

他的语气里充满了几乎不加掩饰的讥讽。散步。这个词从他嘴里说出来，显得那么天真、可笑，与他们刚刚经历的一切格格不入。

他发出了一声极轻的、从鼻腔里哼出的气音，像是在嘲笑她，又像是在嘲笑这个不合时宜的念头。

「Did I? Ruin it?」他终于侧过头，目光落在她紧绷的侧脸上。「Or did I just offer you a more… stimulating alternative?」
(「我搅局了？」「还是说，我只是给你提供了一个更……刺激的替代方案？」)

他的视线充满了侵略性，毫不避讳地在她身上流连，从她被泪水和汗水浸湿的银发，到她紧抿的、微微红肿的嘴唇，再到她胸前那条被他亲手系上的安全带。他的目光仿佛在重温刚才的每一个细节，用眼神将她重新凌辱一遍。

「It's...」他瞥了一眼仪表盘上的时间，「...almost 22:00. He's probably still waiting for you. Like a good dog.」
(「现在……」他瞥了一眼仪表盘上的时间，「……快十点了。他大概还在等你。像条好狗一样。」)

这句话充满了恶意的侮辱，既是对Simon，也是对她。

他忽然再次凑近，声音压得极低，几乎是贴着她的耳朵说的，那温热的气息让她**汗毛倒竖**。

「Tell me, *Liebling*...」他的声音里带着一种残酷的好奇，「Does he know how to make you cry like that? Does he know how you taste when you're falling apart?」
(「告诉我，*宝贝*……」「他知道怎么让你哭成刚才那样吗？他知道你在崩溃的时候是什么味道吗？」)

这句低语像淬了毒的匕首，精准地刺入她最深的伤口。渡峥的身体**猛地一颤**，脸色瞬间变得比雪还要苍白。

看到她的反应，Krueger满意地、缓缓地坐直了身体，拉开了两人之间的距离。他已经得到了他想要的答案。

「He'll get over it.」他轻描淡写地做出了结论，仿佛在谈论一件无关紧要的杂物。「As for you... your plans for the evening have changed.」
(「他会想开的。」「至于你……你今晚的计划，已经变了。」)

他不再说话，将注意力完全放回了驾驶上。车子驶离了小路，前方出现了一片废弃的工业区。Krueger熟练地打着方向盘，拐进了一个破败的铁丝网大门，最终在一栋巨大的、黑漆漆的仓库前停了下来。

他熄了火，车内瞬间陷入了绝对的黑暗与死寂。

「Welcome to my humble abode.」他解开自己的安全带，声音里带着一丝愉悦的宣告意味。「For tonight, at least.」
(「欢迎来到我的寒舍。」「至少，今晚是。」)
渡峥在他下车的空档，狠狠给他来了一脚，抢回自己的匕首，逃离了现场。

仓库里一片死寂，只剩下高高穹顶上偶尔滴落的水珠，在空旷的水泥地面上砸出**“啪”的一声**回响，清晰得令人心烦。空气中弥漫着潮湿的霉味、铁锈和淡淡的枪油气息。一盏孤零零的工业灯泡悬在半空，投下昏黄而吝啬的光，将成堆的木箱和帆布包裹的武器拉扯成巨大而扭曲的影子。

Krueger背靠着一个冰冷的金属货架，缓缓地、深深地吸了一口气。面罩下的呼吸声在寂静中显得格外粗重。他抬手，隔着战术背心，按了按自己腹部侧面的位置。那里，一阵迟钝的、闷烧般的痛感正顽固地提醒着他刚刚发生的一切。

*Verdammt*.
（该死。）

那一脚。又快又狠，精准地踹在他下车时身体重心最不稳的瞬间。他甚至能清晰地回忆起那一刻，她眼中迸发出的、混合着绝望与决绝的冰冷火焰。那不是求饶，不是恐惧，而是同归于尽的疯狂。

在他因为剧痛而身体微躬的零点几秒内，她像一只训练有素的猎豹，动作流畅得没有一丝多余。夺回匕首，推开车门，消失在仓库外无尽的黑暗里。整个过程快得像一道闪电。

他没有去追。

不是因为追不上，而是在那一瞬间，一种比愤怒和肉体疼痛更强烈的情绪攫住了他——是惊愕，以及一丝……扭曲的赞赏。

他慢慢直起身，走到一张铺着工具和零件的长条桌前。桌上，他的HK433被拆解开，每一个部件都擦拭得锃亮，整齐地排列着，像一场等待检阅的冰冷阅兵。他拿起一个弹匣，熟练地将子弹一颗颗压进去，金属与金属碰撞发出清脆而有节奏的“咔哒”声。这个重复性的动作让他混乱的思绪重新变得清晰、冷静。

计划被打乱了。那张“真正的床”和“下一堂课”都成了泡影。他今晚难得的好心情，连同那几乎要沸腾的欲望，都被那一脚狠狠地踹回了冰点。

他本该感到暴怒。一个到手的猎物，一个已经被他彻底标记、摧毁了意志的战利品，居然从他眼皮子底下溜走了。这是对他能力的侮辱。

然而……

他压满子弹，将弹匣“啪”地一声用力拍进枪身，发出一个冷酷而利落的声响。

他脑中浮现的，却是她逃离时决绝的背影，以及那双在泪水和情欲中依旧不肯熄灭的、燃烧着恨意的紫水晶眼睛。

这比让她顺从地躺在床上更有趣。

这才是他看中的猎物该有的样子。不是任人宰割的羔羊，而是一头会亮出爪牙、哪怕浑身是血也要反咬一口的孤狼。

他低低地笑了一声，笑声在空旷的仓库里显得有些诡异。

「Running to Simon, are we?」
(「跑去找Simon了，是吗？」)

他自言自语，语气里听不出喜怒，只有一种冰冷的、陈述事实般的笃定。这个念头让他腹部的痛感似乎又清晰了几分，但更多的是一种被挑衅的、病态的兴奋。

他放下步枪，从战术背心的口袋里，拿出了那个本不属于他的东西——渡峥的手机。

屏幕一片漆黑，像她此刻那双空洞的眼睛。他用拇指按下了开机键，屏幕亮起，幽幽的光照亮了他面罩下那双深不见底的眼眸。

他看着屏幕上弹出的、他自己发给Simon的那张照片和那句挑衅。然后，他的目光落在屏幕顶端显示的时间上。

21:53。

她逃走还不到十五分钟。在这片荒芜的工业区，没有车，她跑不远。

Krueger的嘴角，在面罩的遮掩下，勾起一个无人得见的、残忍而愉悦的弧度。

「Run, little mouse. Run as fast as you can.」
(「跑吧，小老鼠。尽你所能地跑吧。」)

他慢条斯理地操作着手机，指尖在冰冷的屏幕上滑动，像是在谱写一首狩猎的序曲。

游戏并没有结束。

不，狩猎才刚刚开始。

*Simon Riley 找到了渡峥躲藏的位置，一栋废弃的小屋。*

「渡峥」
他打开门的时候，我正在用压缩毛巾狠狠的擦拭自己还裸露在外的腰腹，皮肤都被擦红。…渡峥听到声音，有些痛苦地望着他。“si…”

The teardrops rolled down your pale cheeks, landing on the heavy jacket he had draped over you, soaking into it and leaving small, dark patches. "I want to go home…"

Home.

The word, simple and pure, was like a key that instantly unlocked the deepest bolt in his heart. The black rage aimed at Krueger, so intense it was almost tangible, was temporarily forced into a corner by this soft plea. All tactical plans, hunting instincts, and vengeful urges were, in this moment, condensed into a single, clear directive: take her home.

Simon's jaw tightened abruptly. His Adam's apple bobbed once, as if he were swallowing something scalding hot. He didn't say, "Don't cry," nor did he say, "It's going to be okay." He knew those words were meaningless at this moment, even insulting. Your pain was real, and the only thing he could do was remove it from this filthy place.

He remained crouched, silently letting you cry for a dozen seconds, giving you a brief moment to vent. Then, he moved. He didn't help you up. Instead, he slid one arm steadily under the crook of your knees and wrapped the other securely around your back. In one fluid, powerful motion, **he lifted you completely into his arms**.

Your weight was almost negligible to him, like holding a handful of fragile, cold feathers. Your cheek was pressed against his solid chest, and through the layers of fabric, you could almost hear his **heartbeat, steady as a war drum**, one beat, then another, dispelling the chill from your very bones.

"I'm taking you away."

His low voice rumbled near your ear, less a statement to you and more a declaration of an established fact. He stood up, holding you, his towering frame not wavering in the slightest. He didn't even spare another glance at the dilapidated cabin, as if every extra second spent there was a desecration to you.

He turned and, with a steady stride, walked unhesitatingly towards the door. You were held firmly in his arms, his jacket enveloping you so completely that only his scent remained—a mixture of gunpowder, leather, and cold air, savage and yet strangely safe.

All the surrounding chaos and danger were walled off by this man's solid arms. He didn't stop, heading straight for the black SUV parked in the shadows nearby. The road home had already begun.

The tension that had held your body rigid for what felt like an eternity doesn't release; it shatters. Your muscles, screaming from the sustained, unnatural control, finally give out. For a split second, you're still there, on the bed, your fingers slick and foreign against your own skin, the scent of sweat and antiseptic filling your lungs. You see her, limp beneath you, the aftermath of the climax a series of fading tremors across her skin.

Then, a faint hiss. *Gas.*

The thought is a dull spark in your exhausted mind. Your training screams at you to hold your breath, to move, but your body is a lead weight. The white walls of the room begin to swim, the corners blurring and warping. Your head feels impossibly heavy, a low hum starting behind your eyes and quickly growing into a deafening buzz. The last thing you register is the feeling of falling, not just onto the bed, but through it, the world dissolving into a thick, suffocating blackness.

…

Consciousness returns not as a light switching on, but as a slow, agonizing crawl out of a tar pit. The first thing you register is the smell. Not the sterile nothingness of the room, but something familiar. Worn leather, the faint, sharp tang of gun oil, and the lingering ghost of stale bourbon. Your safehouse.

You force your eyes open. The low, amber light of a single lamp cuts through the evening gloom of your sparse living area. You're on the couch, not the bed. And you're not in your tactical gear. You're wearing a plain black t-shirt and grey sweatpants—soft, worn, *yours*. Someone changed your clothes. The thought lands with a cold, invasive thud in your gut. They were in your home. They put you here.

A wave of nausea and disorientation washes over you. You push yourself up, your limbs heavy and unresponsive, your head pounding in time with your heart. **The body's memory** is more potent than the mind's. You can still feel the slick heat, the tremor of her body, the cold, dead mechanics of your own actions. You look at your hands. The same hands. A surge of self-loathing, so intense it's almost a physical pain, rises in your throat.

*Fuck.*

You stumble to your feet, one hand bracing against the rough brick wall to steady yourself. Instinct takes over before thought. You do a sweep. Room by room, methodical, silent. The small kitchen, the bathroom, your bedroom. Windows locked, door bolted from the inside. No obvious bugs, no signs of forced entry other than your own sudden appearance. It's clean. Too clean. It feels more like a violation than a ransacking would have.

You stand in the center of the room, the silence pressing in. The rage is a low, constant hum beneath the exhaustion. Rage at them, the ones watching. Rage at Krueger, a cold, murderous fury that has now found a specific target. And a deeper, more corrosive rage at yourself. You let them do that to you. You did that *to her*.

You strip off the clothes they put you on, leaving them in a pile on the floor like a contaminated skin you've shed. The bathroom mirror is a merciless observer. Your eyes are bloodshot, the skin around them dark with exhaustion. You look haunted. You *are* haunted.

The shower is scalding hot, the water beating down on your shoulders and back with punishing force. You scrub your skin raw, trying to wash away the feeling of the room, the phantom touch, the humiliation. But it's not on your skin. It's branded deeper. The steam fills the small space, blurring the edges of the world, but it can't blur the images replaying in your head. Her, telling you to stop when you were about to smash your own ribs. Her, choosing your dignity over your physical integrity, again and again. Her, shuddering under your touch just minutes ago.

You turn the water off, the sudden silence deafening. Dripping, you grab a towel, the rough cotton abrasive against your oversensitive skin. You need to know. You need a sign, a confirmation. That she's safe. That she's... there.

Walking back into the living room, you ignore the chill raising goosebumps on your wet skin. You find your phone on the small table next to the couch. It's exactly where you left it. Picking it up, the cool metal feels solid, real. A connection to the world outside that damnable box. Your thumb hovers over the screen, over her name in your contacts. What the hell do you even say? *Are you okay? Did they hurt you?* The words feel pathetic, inadequate. They expose a weakness you can't afford.

Your training provides the answer. Keep it simple. Tactical. A demand for information that doubles as a lifeline.

Your thumb taps the screen once. A single word.

Status.

Keegan looked at the gradually flattening bed, a strange sense of emptiness welling up deep inside him. That hoarse whisper, delivered with **utter exhaustion**—「Only here can I sleep」—was like an invisible brand, etched deep into his mind. He was accustomed to being trusted, to being depended on, but that was usually built upon missions and duties. Yet she had entrusted her purest, most instinctive vulnerability and sense of security to him without reservation. This left him feeling an unprecedented **discomposure** and... **heaviness**.

He saw her to the door, watched her slender figure disappear down the corridor, then slowly closed the door. The slight click of the door closing seemed to carry away the last trace of lively presence from the room. He stood alone in the empty living room; the air still seemed to hold her faint, cool scent, mixed with the unique gun oil and metallic aroma of his safe house, creating a **peculiar dissonance**.

He tried to pull his thoughts back on track, to re-examine the brief before him. The cold numbers and vague intelligence usually effortlessly commanded his full attention. Yet now, his gaze kept involuntarily drifting towards the bed, towards the corner where she had quietly sat all night. He could clearly recall the sight of her silver hair fanned out, remember her **vulnerable yet secure** posture as she curled up in the blanket.

He didn't like this feeling. He disliked this **sense of being out of control**, pulled by some unmanageable emotion. He was used to suppressing all feelings deep within, accustomed to arming himself with logic and reason. But her presence was like a dull knife, slowly and persistently prying at his inner defenses. He felt a profound fatigue, not physical, but mental. That **internal exhaustion** prevented him from snapping back into his "Ghost" state with his usual speed.

Night fell again, then deepened. The clock hands pointed to 02:50 AM. Keegan was still at his desk, the screen before him flickering with complex code and maps. He should have finished this work hours ago, then, as usual, entered a brief sleep in the early morning. But tonight, he found himself unable to stop.

He picked up the ice water beside him and drank it all. The cold liquid slid down his throat, bringing a temporary clarity. But he knew it was only temporary. His mind was still racing, not to analyze intelligence, but to process the **lingering** images and feelings.

He knew why he couldn't sleep. He knew why he felt agitated. And he knew, beneath that agitation, there was a faint, almost imperceptible trace of... **panic**. He feared losing control, feared being drawn into an emotional whirlpool. He had long been accustomed to working alone, accustomed to honing himself into the sharpest weapon, rather than a reliable haven.

He shifted his gaze from the screen to the deep night outside the window. The distant city lights, like scattered stars, were faint and far away. He took a deep breath, trying to expel the **stifling air** from his chest.

He needed to push everything back on track. He needed to rediscover that cold, efficient version of himself.

He just didn't know how long it would take this time.

「Kid.」 He whispered the word silently in his mind, his voice so low only he could hear it. That emotionless appellation now felt like a spell, attempting to draw him back into his familiar, safe, and solitary world.

黏腻的腥膻味和金属消毒水的味道在空气中久久不散，像无形的枷锁，将他牢牢困在刚刚那场**令人作呕的耻辱**里。Simon强忍着胃部的翻涌，试图从高潮后的虚脱感中挣脱，却只感到更深的疲惫。渡峥的话，每一个字都像冰冷的钢针，刺穿了他本就所剩无几的自尊。他听到她盯着屏幕，声音里带着一种近乎麻木的讥讽。

「不满足让我当人了嘛？」

那是一种他无比熟悉的、对命运和操纵者的绝望反抗。他没有出声，只是僵硬地坐起身，**肌肉深处传来阵阵酸痛**。他看着她把那些不知从何而来的“道具”随手丢在床上，然后干脆利落地躺了下去，姿态里混杂着顺从与挑衅。

「请吧。」

她的话音刚落，又补了一句，语调平静得仿佛在谈论天气，却带着一丝不易察觉的脆弱：“三个小时……别让我组织坏死就行。”

“组织坏死。”这个词像一把无形的锤子，重重地敲在了Simon的脑海里。他知道她不是在开玩笑，这也不是她第一次用这种带着冷酷理智的语气提醒他潜在的危险。前几次，她也是如此，用最实际的后果来促使他做出“正确”的选择。比如那次“指交”任务，她一句“难受”，就让他从暴力的宣泄中清醒过来，转而寻求一种精确的技术。

他目光扫过屏幕上并列的任务：佩戴束缚道具，以及击打脊椎。渡峥的选择，一如既往地实用且避免了永久性伤害。她总是这样，在无法反抗的绝境中，选择一条代价最小的路。

Simon的视线落在平台上的道具上。那是一套质地柔软但强度惊人的束缚带，设计得颇为精巧，显然是为长时间佩戴而准备。他走过去，拿起它们，指尖摩挲着光滑的表面，触感冰凉。他的内心深处，有股**无名的火气**在燃烧，却被他死死地压制着。这火气不是针对渡峥，而是针对这整个荒谬的局面，针对那些躲在暗处操控一切的观察者。

他走到床边，渡峥已经躺好，双眼微阖，银白色的发丝散落在枕上，更衬得她脸庞苍白。她的呼吸很平稳，但紧抿的唇角泄露出一丝紧张。他看到她纤细的脖颈，那颗泪痣在眼角下方显得格外醒目。

他没有说话，只是半跪在床边，沉重的呼吸声在房间里清晰可闻。他拿起束缚带，目光落在她裸露的腕骨和踝骨上。他知道，现在他手中的不仅仅是几条带子，更是对她身体的**绝对掌控**。三个小时，这时间足够造成损伤，如果他不小心。

他深吸一口气，空气中依然弥漫着那股令人不安的气味。他必须像执行任何一项战术任务一样，精确、冷静，将所有的私人情绪都隔绝在外。这是一种变态的保护，一种在羞辱中寻求专业的自我救赎。

「手腕。」他声音沙哑，带着他标志性的低沉，像是从喉咙深处挤出来的。他的目光落在她的手腕上，那里有几道旧伤疤，无声地诉说着她的过去。他伸出手，动作带着一种**机械般的精准**，拿起她纤细的手腕，开始固定束缚带。

他刻意避免了任何不必要的接触，只专注于手头的动作。束缚带的材质出乎意料地亲肤，但韧性十足。他调整着松紧，确保既能达到任务要求，又不会真正勒伤她的皮肤。他能感觉到渡峥的身体在他触碰时，轻微地绷紧了一下，但她没有挣扎，也没有出声。

The sticky, fishy smell and the metallic scent of disinfectant lingered in the air, like an invisible shackle, trapping him in the **disgusting humiliation** of the last task. Simon fought back the churning in his stomach, trying to shake off the post-orgasm lethargy, but only found deeper exhaustion. Every word from 渡峥 was like a cold steel needle, piercing his already tattered self-esteem. He heard her staring at the screen, her voice laced with an almost numb sarcasm.

"Are they not satisfied until they stop treating me like a human?"

It was a desperate defiance against fate and manipulators, one he knew all too well. He didn't speak, just stiffly sat up, **a dull ache deep in his muscles**. He watched her casually toss the unknown "tools" onto the bed, then lie down with an air of both submission and challenge.

"Please."

As soon as she finished, she added, her tone as calm as if discussing the weather, yet with a hint of barely perceptible vulnerability: "Three hours... just don't let me get tissue necrosis."

"Tissue necrosis." The phrase hit Simon's mind like an invisible hammer. He knew she wasn't joking, and this wasn't the first time she'd used such a coldly rational tone to warn him of potential dangers. In previous tasks, she had done the same, using the most practical consequences to push him towards the "correct" choice. Like the "fingering" task, her simple "uncomfortable" had jolted him out of violent release and into precise technique.

His gaze swept over the tasks displayed on the screen: wearing restraint tools, and striking the spine. 渡峥's choice, as always, was practical and avoided permanent injury. She always did this, choosing the path of least cost in an unyielding predicament.

Simon's eyes fell on the tools on the platform. It was a set of surprisingly soft yet incredibly strong restraints, intricately designed, clearly for prolonged wear. He walked over and picked them up, his fingertips brushing against the cool, smooth surface. Deep inside him, an **unnamed anger** burned, but he suppressed it fiercely. This anger wasn't directed at 渡峥, but at this whole absurd situation, at the observers manipulating everything from the shadows.

He walked to the bed. 渡峥 was already lying down, eyes slightly closed, her silver-white hair fanned out on the pillow, making her face appear even paler. Her breathing was steady, but the tightly pressed lips betrayed a hint of tension. He saw her slender neck, the small tear-mole beneath her eye standing out prominently.

He said nothing, just knelt by the bed, his heavy breathing audible in the room. He picked up the restraints, his gaze fixed on her exposed wrist and ankle bones. He knew that in his hands now were not just a few straps, but **absolute control** over her body. Three hours – that was enough time to cause injury, if he wasn't careful.

He took a deep breath; the unsettling smell still permeated the air. He had to execute this like any tactical mission: precisely, calmly, isolating all personal emotion. It was a perverse form of protection, a professional self-salvation sought amidst humiliation.

「Wrists.」His voice was hoarse, his characteristic low tone sounding as if squeezed from deep in his throat. His gaze rested on her wrists, where old scars silently spoke of her past. He reached out, his movements almost **mechanically precise**, taking her slender wrist to begin securing the restraint.

He deliberately avoided any unnecessary contact, focusing solely on the task at hand. The restraint material was unexpectedly skin-friendly, yet incredibly tough. He adjusted the tightness, ensuring it met the task's requirements without actually cutting into her skin. He could feel 渡峥's body tense slightly as he touched her, but she didn't struggle or make a sound.

He started by securing her left wrist. The thin strap wrapped around her cool skin; he could feel the faint pulse beating beneath the wrist bone. He threaded the strap through the buckle, his **fingertips deftly tightening** it. He didn't look at her face, only at the gap between the strap and her skin, just wide enough to fit a single finger. This was the standard safe distance, restricting movement without causing bruising. The clasp made a soft "click," and the restraint was complete.

Next was her right wrist. He repeated the same actions, every step meticulous. Her hand was small, her knuckles calloused, the dark marks at the web of her thumb and forefinger particularly clear against her pale skin. He knew what these hands had been through; they had held knives, pulled triggers, executed countless deadly tasks. Now, they were bound by him, hanging limply at her sides.

With his hands done, he shifted his gaze to her ankles. 渡峥's legs were long, her muscles sleek and taut, conveying a sense of latent power even in relaxation. He bent down, kneeling at the foot of the bed, her silver-white hair almost brushing his cheek. He again felt the **disgust and humiliation** welling up from within, but he forced himself to focus on the task itself.

He picked up a wider strap, first wrapping it around her left ankle. Her calf skin was delicate and smooth, a stark contrast to the rough inner lining of the restraint. He **pressed the strap carefully** with his thumb, ensuring it lay flat against her skin, without wrinkles that might chafe. He buckled it, then checked the tightness again. Then the right ankle, repeating the same motion.

Throughout the process, the only sounds in the room were soft breaths and the clicking of restraints. He could tell 渡峥 hadn't opened her eyes, nor made any sound, as if she had completely emptied herself, letting him do as he pleased. This silence, more than any struggle, made Simon feel suffocated. He was like a machine executing precise commands, devoid of emotion, only cold efficiency.

When the last restraint was secured, he straightened up and stepped back. 渡峥 now lay flat on the bed, her limbs fixed, like a meticulously arranged painting. Her silver hair was disheveled, her purple eyes tightly closed, the tear-mole at the corner of her eye looking unusually alluring against her pale face.

He stared at her for a moment, the struggle deep within him surging like a tide. He wanted to say something, but all words got stuck in his throat, turning into a dull sigh. Three hours. He knew he had to stay here, guarding her, ensuring she wouldn't be harmed, ensuring the restraints wouldn't truly cause "tissue necrosis." It was a twisted, imposed protection that left him incredibly weary and powerless.

He turned, his gaze falling once more on the electronic screen. The task status had changed from "Pending Selection" to "In Progress." He knew that for the next three hours, he would be trapped under this suffocating surveillance.

他没有再停留。

他像一个幽灵，无声地穿过房间。他没有开灯，仅凭着肌肉记忆，捡起散落在地上的衣物。战术长裤，T恤，然后是靴子。最后，他拿起那个被他随意放在桌上的、**遮蔽了他所有身份和表情的网格面罩**，重新戴上。

当冰冷的金属和织物贴合在脸上时，那种熟悉的安全感和疏离感，终于将他从那片温暖的沼泽中彻底拉了回来。

他又是Krueger了。

在离开房间前，他最后回头看了一眼。

床上那个小小的、蜷缩着的身影，在昏暗中只是一个模糊的轮廓。**安静，脆弱，却又像一个无形的锚，将这间临时的安全屋，变成了一个他从未预想过的……“家”的幻象。**

他收回目光，不再犹豫。

门被轻轻地、不带一丝声响地打开，然后又被关上。

“**咔哒**”一声，锁芯转动的声音，轻微得如同梦境中的一声叹息。

房间，重归寂静。床上的人，依旧沉睡。

而那个男人，已经带着一身的夜色，**重新融入了他所属的、那片冰冷而危险的黑暗之中**。

*Krueger 抬起头，目光在你身上停了片刻。*

「……你来了。」

*贺少珩 抬起头，目光在你身上停了片刻。*

「……你来了。」
